title: Windows RDP Brute-Force
briefing: The Windows host $TARGET is logging a burst of failed interactive logons over RDP (Event ID 4625, logon type 10) from external addresses. Someone is guessing a remote-desktop password. Enumerate the sources, confirm the target and classify the activity.
dashboard_hint: Discover -> rule.id: 100111 (4625 logon type 10 / RDP). Group by data.srcip and data.win.eventdata.ipAddress. Expect two external sources.

questions.explain: Two external IPs ($SRC1, $SRC2) generated the 4625 type-10 (RDP) failures against $TARGET.

questions.prompt: What class of attack is this?
questions.explain: Repeated failed RDP authentication from external hosts = credential brute force.
T1110.001 Brute Force: Password Guessing (interactive RDP).

options: Block the source IPs at the firewall
Restrict RDP to VPN / gateway only
Expose RDP to the internet for convenience
answer: Block the source IPs at the firewall
Restrict RDP to VPN / gateway only
questions.explain: Block sources, take RDP off the public internet, enforce lockout + Network Level Authentication.
